metadata.name: Young Sheldon
metadata.description: 90-question trivia pack covering all seasons of the Young Sheldon TV show — locations, pop culture, family milestones, production details, science, and sports.
metadata.author: Trivial World Team

questions.questionText: In what fictional East Texas town does the Cooper family live in Young Sheldon?
questions.tidbits: East Texas, where Young Sheldon is set, is characterized by its conservative culture, religious communities, and distinct regional identity.

questions.questionText: What state is Medford, Texas located in?
questions.tidbits: Medford, Texas (Sheldon's fictional hometown) is depicted as a small town where everyone knows each other and Sheldon stands out dramatically.

questions.questionText: What is the name of the high school where George Cooper Sr. coaches and Sheldon attends?
questions.tidbits: The University of East Texas (fictional) in the show is based loosely on real East Texas universities like UT Tyler.

questions.questionText: What university does Sheldon commute to for college classes in the early seasons?
What is the name of the church that Mary Cooper is devoted to and frequently drags her family to?
What neighboring town does the show occasionally reference as a rival to Medford?
The show is set starting in what year, when Sheldon is 9 years old? A) 1985, B) 1987, C) 1989, D) 1991
What is the name of the bar that Meemaw frequently visits and eventually helps run?
The Cooper home sits in a typical East Texas suburb. What type of housing style is the Cooper family home?
After leaving Medford, Sheldon moves to what California city to attend Caltech?
What is the name of the Cooper family's friendly neighbor whose son Billy is Missy's classmate?
What fast-food restaurant does the Cooper family treat as a special outing, reflecting 1980s-90s Texas life?
What is the name of the local grocery store referenced in Medford life?
In later seasons, Sheldon and Mary travel to what Texas capital city for a university-related academic event?
What is the name of the adult neighbor who is Mary's closest friend and confidant throughout the series?
What iconic science fiction TV show is Sheldon's absolute favorite, influencing nearly every aspect of his life?
questions.tidbits: Jim Parsons narrates Young Sheldon as the adult Sheldon looking back, directly connecting it to The Big Bang Theory timeline.

questions.questionText: Young Sheldon is a prequel spin-off of what long-running CBS sitcom?
questions.tidbits: The show is set in 1989–1993, requiring meticulous period-accurate props, clothing, and pop culture references.

questions.questionText: Who provides the adult Sheldon narration voiceover throughout the series?
questions.tidbits: Georgie Cooper, Sheldon's older brother, runs a tire shop as an adult in The Big Bang Theory — his early entrepreneurial instincts are shown in Young Sheldon.

questions.questionText: What Star Trek captain does Sheldon most frequently reference or admire?
What science-fiction film franchise does Sheldon dismiss as inferior to Star Trek?
Missy Cooper enjoys watching what popular soap opera that irritates Sheldon?
What wrestling program do Georgie and George Sr. bond over watching on TV?
Sheldon becomes obsessed with what video game console that was cutting-edge in 1989-1990?
What classic monster movie does Sheldon watch and critique for its scientific inaccuracies?
Sheldon idolizes what real-life physicist as his ultimate hero, referencing him throughout the series?
What network originally aired Young Sheldon in the United States?
The show's opening theme song is called what?
The Young Sheldon series finale directly sets up what achievement that adult Sheldon and Amy win in The Big Bang Theory?
What popular tabletop roleplaying game does Sheldon attempt to introduce to his family?
Mary Cooper is devoted to watching what televangelist on TV?
How old is Sheldon Cooper when Young Sheldon begins? A) 7, B) 8, C) 9, D) 10
questions.tidbits: Young Sheldon premiered in 2017 and ran for 7 seasons, concluding in 2024 with Sheldon leaving for Caltech.

questions.questionText: What grade does Sheldon enter at Medford High School at the start of the series?
questions.tidbits: Iain Armitage was 9 years old when he began playing young Sheldon, and his performance was praised for capturing Jim Parsons's mannerisms.

questions.questionText: What is the name of Sheldon's twin sister?
questions.tidbits: The show filmed in California but carefully recreated the look of 1980s–90s Texas through set design and location shoots.

questions.questionText: What is the name of Sheldon's older brother?
What is the nickname for Sheldon's paternal grandmother who lives nearby?
What milestone does Sheldon achieve when he begins attending East Texas Tech, separating him from his high school peers?
questions.answerText: Attending college while still a child

questions.questionText: In the final season, what major life event happens to George Cooper Sr. that was foreshadowed throughout the series?
What business does Georgie eventually own and operate by the end of the series?
Meemaw begins a long-term romantic relationship with what recurring character who is also Sheldon's physics mentor?
What is the first name of Meemaw's (Constance Tucker's) rival poker player who appears in later seasons?
What religion does Mary Cooper practice devoutly, causing tension with the scientific Sheldon?
In what season does Georgie get a girl pregnant, a storyline that connects to the Big Bang Theory timeline?
What personal milestone does Missy reach in later seasons as she grows more independent from Sheldon?
What is George Cooper Sr.'s primary job at Medford High School?
What Big Bang Theory myth about George Sr. does the Young Sheldon finale directly address and reframe for viewers?
Young Sheldon uses what visual device at the start of episodes to establish time and place?
questions.tidbits: Montana Jordan, who plays Georgie, became one of the show's most popular characters despite being introduced as a secondary figure.

questions.questionText: What camera style does Young Sheldon use, distinguishing it visually from The Big Bang Theory?
questions.tidbits: The Cooper family's home set is designed to feel authentically middle-class 1980s American with period-accurate decor.

questions.questionText: Unlike The Big Bang Theory, Young Sheldon deliberately omits what standard sitcom audio element?
questions.tidbits: Meemaw (Connie Tucker) is portrayed as the fun-loving, gambling, sometimes morally ambiguous counterpoint to Sheldon's structured worldview.

questions.questionText: What era-defining interior design feature is prominently visible on the walls of the Cooper home?
The opening title sequence evokes nostalgia using what photo style common in the 1980s?
Sheldon's bedroom walls are decorated with posters representing his two main obsessions. Name both subjects.
What visual running gag involves Sheldon wearing specific shirts tied to what kind of schedule?
Young Sheldon's use of adult voiceover narration mirrors the structure of what beloved 1980s coming-of-age TV show?
What is typically written on the chalkboard visible in scenes where Sheldon studies at home?
What decade's aesthetic is faithfully recreated in the show's costumes, sets, and props?
The exterior establishing shot of the Cooper house typically features what regional tree species in the yard?
George Sr.'s recliner chair is a recurring prop symbol. What is he almost always doing while seated in it?
What religious decorative prop appears consistently on the kitchen wall of the Cooper home?
How does the visual design of Meemaw's home contrast with the tidy Cooper household?
The Cooper living room features a period-accurate television. What general type of TV set is it?
What branch of physics is Sheldon Cooper most passionate about?
questions.tidbits: Sheldon's early interest in train schedules and precise time management shown in the series foreshadows his adult obsession with routine in The Big Bang Theory.

questions.questionText: What IQ number is Sheldon stated to have in the show? A) 167, B) 175, C) 187, D) 200
questions.tidbits: The series shows Sheldon's early experiments with physics including his "Cooper Coupler" train experiment.

questions.questionText: What famous equation by Einstein does Sheldon frequently reference as foundational to all physics?
questions.tidbits: Einstein's E=mc² established that mass and energy are interchangeable — a concept central to nuclear energy and the atomic bomb. Sheldon frequently invokes it as the foundation of all modern physics, even when it has little direct relevance to what he's explaining.

questions.questionText: Sheldon is fascinated by what theoretical framework that attempts to unify all forces of nature?
Dr. John Sturgis holds a PhD in what specific scientific field?
Sheldon attempts to build a nuclear reactor in his garage. What radioactive element was he trying to obtain to fuel it?
What mathematical concept does Sheldon explain to his family using everyday analogies in an early episode?
At East Texas Tech, what level of academic degree is Sheldon pursuing during the series?
What famous quantum mechanics thought experiment involving a cat does Sheldon explain to his confused family?
Sheldon uses his beloved model train sets to explain what fundamental physics concept to others?
What is the full name of Sheldon's physics professor and long-term mentor at East Texas Tech?
Sheldon graduates high school at what remarkably young age? A) 9, B) 11, C) 13, D) 14
What physics concept, related to his eventual Nobel Prize, does Sheldon begin developing ideas about during the series?
What logical framework does Sheldon invoke when debating the existence of God with his devout mother Mary?
What specific number does Sheldon declare to be the best number, a trait he carries into The Big Bang Theory? A) 42, B) 73, C) 97, D) 137
What sport does George Cooper Sr. coach at Medford High School?
questions.tidbits: High school football coaching in Texas carries enormous community prestige, and the show uses George Sr.'s position to highlight how out of place Sheldon feels in a culture defined by athletic achievement over academic brilliance.

questions.questionText: What position does Georgie primarily play on the Medford High football team?
questions.tidbits: Sheldon's distaste for sports, especially football, contrasts sharply with his father's deep passion for coaching, creating ongoing family tension.

questions.questionText: What rival town's team does Medford High regularly compete against in football?
questions.tidbits: The show accurately depicts the cultural dominance of high school football in Texas, where Friday night games are community events.

questions.questionText: What sport does Missy take an interest in to prove herself outside of Sheldon's academic shadow?
Georgie and George Sr. bond primarily over watching what professional sport together on TV?
What card game does Meemaw teach the Cooper children, reflecting her love of gambling?
George Sr. takes Sheldon to a professional game in an attempt to bond. Which NFL team do they go see?
What PE class physical activity does Sheldon catastrophically fail at due to his complete lack of athletic ability?
Meemaw regularly hosts games of what at her home, revealing her gambling streak to the kids?
Sheldon attempts to apply what mathematical academic discipline to board games during Cooper family game night?
What is the last name of the Cooper family's neighbors whose son Billy is Missy's friend?
Texas high school football games are traditionally held on what night of the week, a cultural touchstone shown in the series?
Sheldon attempts to find meaning in what American sport by reducing it entirely to statistics and probability?
What family recreational activity do the Coopers participate in together in a group outing episode?
Though Sheldon excels academically, Georgie's football athleticism earns what specific emotional response from George Sr.?
